ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn entry exists specifically for packaged snow pear drink; closest defensible match is pear juice food product (FOODON_03301710). Snow pear (Pyrus bretschneideri) is a specific cultivar; the packaged drink is a processed, sweetened beverage derived from snow pear.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP (Pear juice) is the closest defensible match. The product is a packaged, sweetened snow pear beverage rather than pure pear juice; sugar content (11g/100g) indicates added sugar beyond natural pear juice levels.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for packaged snow pear drink; SNOMED CT does not maintain granular codes for this beverage subtype.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical single-serve portion of packaged snow pear drink is one can or bottle of approximately 250 ml (250 g), providing around 115 kcal and 27.5 g of sugar per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Snow Pear Drink (Packaged) — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Snow Pear Drink (Packaged), cr_id D05010036, with confidence 0.95. All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for packaged snow pear drink in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 55 based on the closest analogous food: sweetened fruit juice drinks (GI 45–65 range) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Apple juice unsweetened GI ~41–44; adjustment upward to ~55 for a sweetened pear-based beverage with 11g sugar/100g and zero dietary fibre is defensible. GI 55 falls at the upper boundary of the low category (low: ≤55). Glycemic load per 250g serving = (55 × 11.5 × 2.5) / 100 = 15.8g. Estimation basis documented per Veritas GI policy.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content (11g/100g) in liquid form; sodium is low at 12mg/100g but does not offset sugar-driven hypertension risk
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this sugar-sweetened beverage; the 11g/100g sugar load exceeds the AHA/WHO 5g/100g beverage sugar threshold and promotes insulin-mediated sodium retention and elevated blood pressure independent of the low sodium content — choose water, unsweetened herbal tea, or fresh whole snow pear instead.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 11g sugar/100g and zero dietary fibre; rapid glucose absorption with no moderating nutrients
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with type 2 diabetes should avoid this packaged snow pear drink entirely; the 11g/100g sugar in liquid form is absorbed rapidly, causing acute postprandial glucose spikes, and the complete absence of dietary fibre, protein, and fat provides no glycaemic buffering — unsweetened beverages or fresh whole snow pear with intact fibre are strongly preferred.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High free sugar intake from a sugar-sweetened beverage linked to elevated triglycerides, visceral adiposity, and CVD risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk should limit or avoid this packaged snow pear drink; regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages at 11g sugar/100g is associated with elevated serum triglycerides, increased visceral fat, and higher CVD event risk per WHO and AHA dietary guidelines — water or unsweetened beverages are the recommended alternative.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Snow Pear Drink (Packaged) is a ready-to-drink, commercially produced Asian beverage made from snow pear (Pyrus bretschneideri) juice or concentrate, water, and added sugar. It is widely sold in Singapore and across Southeast Asia in canned or bottled form, typically in 250–330 ml single-serve portions. The drink has a light, mildly sweet flavour with a subtle floral pear note and a thin, watery texture characteristic of diluted fruit juice drinks. It is commonly consumed as a refreshing beverage and is associated in traditional Chinese culture with cooling properties. Nutritionally, per 100g it provides 46 kcal, 11.5g carbohydrate (of which 11g is sugar), 0g protein, 0g fat, 0g dietary fibre, and 12mg sodium. Protein and fat are naturally zero as this is a sugar-water-based fruit beverage with no protein or fat-containing ingredients; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database. The high free sugar content (11g/100g) with zero fibre classifies this as a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB), which is clinically significant: SSBs are independently associated with elevated postprandial blood glucose, insulin resistance, hypertriglyceridaemia, elevated blood pressure via insulin-mediated sodium retention, and increased cardiovascular disease risk. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low, at the upper boundary of the low GI range) based on analogous sweetened fruit juice drinks from the University of Sydney GI Database, with a glycaemic load of approximately 15.8 per 250g serving. This product is not suitable for individuals with type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease risk. It is vegetarian, vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free, and is considered halal.
common_user_queries: Is packaged snow pear drink suitable for diabetics?
How much sugar is in snow pear drink?
Can I drink snow pear drink if I have high blood pressure?
What is the glycemic index of snow pear drink?
Is snow pear drink a sugar-sweetened beverage?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id D05010036) with a reported confidence of 0.95. The food is a commercially standardised packaged product with consistent formulation. GI is an analogous estimate (medium confidence for GI component only) but all core macros and secondary nutrients are exact from an approved primary source.
notes: protein_g is naturally zero as this is a sugar-water-based fruit beverage containing no protein-contributing ingredients; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database. fat_g is naturally zero by product formulation — no fat-containing ingredients are present; confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero consistent with zero total fat. dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore Foodo database — the processing of pear juice/concentrate removes the fibre present in whole snow pear fruit. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed micronutrient, fatty acid, and moisture data for this packaged beverage are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for D05010036 as of 2026. GI value of 55 is an analogous estimate based on sweetened fruit juice drinks from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for packaged snow pear drink was found. GI 55 is at the upper boundary of the low GI category (low: ≤55); gi_category is set to 'low' accordingly. Ontology codes use approximate matches: FoodOn FOODON_03301710 (pear juice food product) and FoodEx2 A0FKP (Pear juice) are the closest defensible anchors for this packaged sweetened pear beverage.
